en.commentary: The Dell UltraSharp U2724DE is the monitor I recommend to almost everyone, and the reason is specific: its IPS Black panel reaches a 2,000:1 contrast ratio while a single Thunderbolt 4 cable runs video, 90W charging, and a full port hub. That turns one screen into your entire docking station, which is exactly what a hybrid desk needs. For people who want every pixel sharp, the U2725QE pushes the same idea to 4K at 120Hz with a world-first 3,000:1 contrast IPS Black panel, and at $700 it is the smartest 4K work display of the year. The ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM earns the image-quality crown with its 4K 240Hz QD-OLED panel and DisplayPort 2.1a UHBR20 carrying uncompressed 4K, the screen creators and gamers reach for when only the best will do. Samsung's Odyssey OLED G8 takes the premium entertainment lane with a 32-inch 4K 240Hz QD-OLED and a built-in smart hub. Color professionals get the ASUS ProArt PA279CRV, factory-calibrated to Delta E under 2 with 99% DCI-P3 and 99% Adobe RGB at a price that undercuts every rival. For raw value, the Gigabyte M28U remains the 4K bargain that started it all, a 28-inch 144Hz IPS panel with HDMI 2.1 near $450. Mac owners split between the Apple Studio Display, whose 5K 218 PPI panel and Thunderbolt integration feel native, and the LG UltraFine 5K that pioneered the look. When budget rules, the Acer KB272 delivers a 27-inch 1080p IPS panel at 100Hz for around $130, the cleanest entry point in the category.

highlights.title: The U2724DE wins because one cable replaces your entire dock
highlights.body: I keep ranking the U2724DE first because it solves the real problem on a modern desk: cable clutter and a missing dock. Thunderbolt 4 carries the 1440p 120Hz signal, delivers 90W to charge a laptop, and feeds an Ethernet jack plus a row of USB ports, all from one connection. The IPS Black panel backs that convenience with a 2,000:1 contrast ratio, so blacks look genuinely deep for an LCD. For a hybrid worker who docks and undocks daily, nothing else this year is as effortless.

highlights.title: OLED owns the high end, and the PG27UCDM proves it
highlights.body: The image-quality gap between QD-OLED and LCD at the top of the market is now decisive. The PG27UCDM runs a 27-inch 4K panel at 240Hz with per-pixel light control and a 0.03ms response time, and its DisplayPort 2.1a UHBR20 port carries that 4K 240Hz signal completely uncompressed. Add a tandem panel design that improves burn-in longevity and a proximity sensor that blanks the screen when you step away, and you get the display I would put in front of any creator or gamer chasing the best picture.

highlights.title: The Gigabyte M28U is still the 4K value benchmark
highlights.body: Years after launch, the M28U remains the monitor I point budget-minded 4K buyers toward. A 28-inch SS IPS panel at 144Hz with HDMI 2.1, sub-1 Delta E color accuracy, and a built-in KVM near $450 still embarrasses pricier rivals on dollars-per-feature. It gives up true HDR and OLED contrast, and that is the honest trade, but for a sharp, fast 4K panel that handles work and gaming, the value is unmatched.

highlights.title: Color work belongs to the ProArt PA279CRV at this price
highlights.body: The ProArt PA279CRV delivers what photographers and video editors actually need: factory calibration to Delta E under 2, 99% DCI-P3 and 99% Adobe RGB coverage, and a hardware calibration workflow that holds its look across long sessions. USB-C with daisy-chaining keeps a multi-monitor desk tidy. At $469 it undercuts every comparable creator display, which is why I rank it as the color pick over options costing twice as much.

highlights.title: 1440p at 27 inches is the resolution sweet spot for 2026
highlights.body: The data keeps pointing to the same conclusion: a 27-inch 1440p panel hits roughly 109 PPI, sharp enough that text and UI look crisp without forcing display scaling, and easy enough to drive that mid-range GPUs hold high frame rates. That is why my top all-rounder is a 1440p panel rather than a 4K one. 4K earns its place for creators and detail-obsessed users, and 1440p remains the resolution most people should buy.

zh-tw.commentary: 老實說，要我跟身邊的人推薦一台螢幕，我九成會直接講 Dell UltraSharp U2724DE，理由很具體。它的 IPS Black 面板做到 2,000:1 對比，黑得夠深，重點是一條 Thunderbolt 4 線就能同時吃影像、90W 供電還有整排擴充埠，等於把螢幕變成一個完整的擴充底座，這正是現在在家跟公司兩邊跑的混合辦公族最需要的。想要每個像素都更銳利的，U2725QE 把同樣的思路推到 4K 120Hz，還是全球首款 3,000:1 對比的 IPS Black 面板，台幣兩萬出頭，是今年最聰明的 4K 工作螢幕。畫質王座我給 ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM，27 吋 4K 240Hz QD-OLED 配上 DP 2.1a UHBR20 的零壓縮輸出，創作者跟玩家想要極致畫面時就是它。三星 Odyssey OLED G8 走高階娛樂路線，32 吋 4K 240Hz QD-OLED 還內建智慧聯網。專業修圖調色的就看 ASUS ProArt PA279CRV，原廠校色 Delta E 小於 2，99% DCI-P3 加 99% Adobe RGB，價格還壓在同級最低。講純性價比，技嘉 M28U 還是那台開創 4K 平價路線的代表作，28 吋 144Hz IPS 配 HDMI 2.1，台幣一萬五出頭就有。用 Mac 的人就在 Apple Studio Display 跟 LG UltraFine 5K 之間挑，前者 5K 218 PPI 配 Thunderbolt 整合最順手。預算有限就 Acer KB272，27 吋 1080p IPS 100Hz，台幣四千有找，入門最乾淨的選擇。

highlights.title: U2724DE 強在一條線取代你整個擴充底座
highlights.body: 我一直把 U2724DE 排第一，是因為它解掉了現代桌面真正的痛點，線材一團亂加上缺一個 dock。Thunderbolt 4 一條線同時傳 1440p 120Hz 畫面、供 90W 幫筆電充電，還帶一個網路孔跟一排 USB，全部靠這一條搞定。IPS Black 面板做到 2,000:1 對比，以 LCD 來說黑階真的夠深。對每天插拔筆電在家跟公司兩邊跑的人來說，今年沒有比它更省事的選擇。

highlights.title: 高階畫質這局 OLED 全拿，PG27UCDM 就是證明
highlights.body: 在市場頂端，QD-OLED 跟 LCD 的畫質差距現在已經拉開了。PG27UCDM 是 27 吋 4K 面板跑 240Hz，每個像素獨立控光，反應時間 0.03ms，而且 DP 2.1a UHBR20 那個埠能把 4K 240Hz 訊號完全零壓縮送出去。再加上 tandem 面板設計拉長了抗烙印壽命，還有人離開就自動黑屏的距離感測器，這就是我會推給任何追求最佳畫面的創作者跟玩家的那一台。

highlights.title: 技嘉 M28U 到現在還是 4K 性價比的標竿
highlights.body: 上市這麼多年，要我推薦預算取向的 4K 螢幕，M28U 還是首選。28 吋 SS IPS 面板跑 144Hz 配 HDMI 2.1，色準 Delta E 小於 1，還內建 KVM，台幣一萬五出頭，這個每塊錢買到的規格還是電爆更貴的對手。它在真 HDR 跟 OLED 對比上有取捨，這點我老實講，但要一塊銳利又快的 4K 面板兼顧工作跟遊戲，性價比就是沒人打得贏。

highlights.title: 這個價位談調色，ProArt PA279CRV 沒有對手
highlights.body: ProArt PA279CRV 給的正是攝影師跟剪片的人真正要的東西，原廠校色 Delta E 小於 2，99% DCI-P3 加 99% Adobe RGB 覆蓋，還有硬體校色流程能讓畫面在長時間工作下維持一致。USB-C 串接讓多螢幕桌面保持乾淨。台幣一萬三出頭就壓過所有同級創作者螢幕的價格，這也是為什麼我把它擺在調色首選，連貴一倍的機種都比不過。

highlights.title: 27 吋配 1440p 是 2026 年解析度的甜蜜點
highlights.body: 資料一直指向同一個結論，27 吋 1440p 面板大概是 109 PPI，文字跟介面夠銳利，不用開縮放就很清楚，而且中階顯卡就能輕鬆推高幀數。所以我心目中的最佳全能機是 1440p 而不是 4K。4K 在創作者跟對細節龜毛的人身上有它的價值，但 1440p 還是大多數人最該買的解析度。

en.commentary: The Dell U2724DE keeps my top spot, and after living with it I keep coming back to the same conclusion: for the way most people actually work, this is the best monitor you can buy. The IPS Black panel gives you deep contrast, the ergonomic stand and rich connectivity including a built-in KVM and USB-C hub make a cluttered desk disappear, and the price stays reasonable for what you get. Its sibling, the Dell U2725QE, holds second by adding a sharper 4K panel for people who want more pixel density without giving up that same brilliant productivity feature set. The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays third as the image-quality standout, that QD-OLED panel is genuinely stunning for gaming and HDR content. Samsung's Odyssey OLED G8 follows in fourth on the strength of its gorgeous panel. This week the notable launch chatter is around Dell's new 2026 UltraSharp QD-OLED models like the U3226Q, a reference-grade 32-inch 4K panel with a built-in colorimeter aimed at color-critical pros. It is exciting, but at around $2,600 it serves a narrow audience, and it has not displaced my mainstream picks. So I am keeping the order steady. My advice: most people should buy the U2724DE and enjoy the KVM and clean desk. Want 4K sharpness, step up to the U2725QE. Gamers and HDR fans should go for the PG27UCDM.

highlights.body: The IPS Black panel, ergonomic stand, and built-in KVM plus USB-C hub make it the productivity monitor I recommend to most people.

highlights.body: It keeps the same brilliant feature set while stepping up to a crisper 4K panel for those who want more pixel density.

highlights.body: The QD-OLED panel is genuinely stunning for games and HDR content, my pick when image quality comes first.

zh-tw.commentary: Dell U2724DE 守住我心中第一名，實際用了一陣子，我還是回到同一個結論：以多數人真正的工作方式來看，這就是你能買到最好的螢幕。IPS Black 面板給你很深的對比，符合人體工學的腳架加上豐富的連接埠，內建 KVM 跟 USB-C 集線器，桌面瞬間清爽。以這個內容來說，價格也很合理。它的兄弟款 Dell U2725QE 排第二，多了更銳利的 4K 面板，給想要更高像素密度、又不想放棄這套出色生產力功能的人。Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM 守住第三，是畫質標竿，那塊 QD-OLED 面板拿來打電動跟看 HDR 內容真的驚豔。Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 排第四，靠的是它那塊漂亮面板。這週比較受矚目的新品話題，是 Dell 2026 新款 UltraSharp QD-OLED，像 U3226Q，一塊參考級 32 吋 4K 面板，內建校色儀，主打對色彩要求極高的專業人士。很令人興奮，但定價約台幣八萬，服務的客群很窄，也還沒撼動我的主流推薦。所以排名維持不動。建議：多數人買 U2724DE，享受 KVM 跟清爽桌面就好。想要 4K 銳利度，升級到 U2725QE。玩家跟 HDR 愛好者就挑 PG27UCDM。

en.commentary: The monitor scene is busy with new OLED arrivals, but my everyday picks hold firm because the best all rounders still come from Dell's UltraSharp line. The U2724DE keeps my top spot as the productivity workhorse most people should buy. Its 120Hz IPS panel feels smooth for daily work, the built in KVM and full port array clean up a two machine desk, and the ergonomic stand makes long sessions easy on the neck. For a single monitor that handles spreadsheets, code, and a bit of evening gaming, it is the safe answer. The U2725QE sits second for anyone who wants the sharper 4K resolution and tighter text, and it shares the same excellent connectivity. The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM holds third as the image quality champion, with a QD-OLED panel that delivers reference level color and stunning contrast for creators and gamers who put the picture first. This month brought a wave of new releases, including Dell's pro grade U3226Q QD-OLED with a built in colorimeter and fresh Asus 4K 240Hz W-OLED panels, so the high end keeps getting deeper. I held the ranks because those premium arrivals serve niches rather than unseating my mainstream picks. Buy the U2724DE for the best all around productivity, step up to the U2725QE for 4K sharpness, and choose the PG27UCDM if image quality is your single biggest priority.

en.commentary: The Dell U2724DE stays my top all-rounder this week, and the reason is the docking. A single USB-C cable charges your laptop, drives the panel, and lights up its 2.5G Ethernet and downstream ports, which turns a desk into a one-cable workstation. The 120Hz IPS panel is sharp and color-solid for everything short of pro photo work, and at its price the value is hard to beat. The U2725QE holds second for anyone who wants the same dock in 4K. On the gaming side, the ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays third as the QD-OLED to buy, pairing 4K with 240Hz for the cleanest motion I have tested at this size. ASUS shipped the PG32UCWM and XG32UQWMS 4K 240Hz W-OLED panels in early June, and Samsung is showing a 32-inch triple-mode QD-OLED that switches between 4K 360Hz and 1080p 680Hz, both worth watching, but neither is broadly available yet so I held all ten ranks steady this week. The clear trend is OLED maturing fast at 27 and 32 inches while prices ease. If you work all day, buy the Dell dock monitors. If you game, the ASUS and Samsung Odyssey OLED panels deliver the motion clarity and contrast that an IPS simply cannot match. For color work, the ProArt and BenQ stay the calibrated picks.

en.commentary: I keep coming back to the same conclusion this week, the Dell U2724DE is still the monitor I recommend to the most people. The 120Hz IPS panel, the USB-C dock with 90W passthrough, and the slim bezels make it the easiest single-cable desk upgrade I know of, and after living with one for months I trust the ergonomics completely. For pure image quality though, the ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM has earned a small bump from me. Reviewers at RTINGS now call its 27-inch 4K QD-OLED panel the best they have tested, and the fresh wave of Computex coverage backs that up. The contrast and motion clarity are genuinely in another tier, so I nudged its score up a notch. The Dell U2725QE remains my pick for anyone who wants 4K sharpness on a productivity panel, the extra pixels pay off the moment you open a spreadsheet or a timeline. Computex 2026 brought a flood of mini LED announcements from TCL, including the 27C2A Pro that shipped on June 5, and I expect those to reshape the value end of this list later in the year. For now I am holding the field steady because nothing I have measured changes the order. If you do color work, the ASUS ProArt PA279CRV still gives you factory calibration at a price that feels almost unfair. Buy for the panel type that matches your actual work, that is the advice that ages best.

en.commentary: Dell U2724DE stays my top pick for the office and the hybrid desk. The 120Hz IPS panel, full Thunderbolt docking, and the cleanest ergonomics in the category still make it the monitor I recommend to most people who work all day at a desk. One cable for power, data, and display keeps a setup tidy in a way few rivals match. The U2725QE holds second as the 4K step-up for anyone who wants more pixels for text and detail work without giving up that same docking and stand. The two Dells are close, so the choice really comes down to whether you need 4K sharpness or prefer the value of the 1440p sibling. Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM keeps third and remains my image-quality champion. That 4K QD-OLED panel is reference-grade for gaming and HDR, and nothing here matches its contrast. Worth noting for creators, Dell's incoming UltraSharp U3226Q QD-OLED signals where premium professional panels are heading, with Dolby Vision and 99% DCI-P3, so the OLED productivity gap is closing fast. Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 and Asus ProArt PA279CRV round out the upper middle, the first for gamers and the second for color-accurate creative work at a fair price. The order is unchanged this week. The big news was announcements rather than units you can buy and rank today, so I held the board steady.

en.commentary: The Dell U2724DE holds my top spot as the best all-around monitor for most people, and nothing this week changed that. Its 1440p IPS Black panel, full Thunderbolt 4 hub, 120Hz refresh and superb ergonomics make it the most complete productivity display you can buy without overspending. For a home office or a hybrid work setup, this is still the first monitor I recommend. The bigger story this week is fresh deep-dive coverage of the Dell U2725QE, the 4K sibling that sits at number two. RTINGS and others confirm it is Dell's first enhanced IPS Black panel, hitting a measured 2933:1 contrast at 58% brightness, which is exceptional for IPS and delivers genuinely deep blacks. It covers 100% sRGB, 95% Adobe RGB and 92% DCI-P3 with an out-of-box dE average of 0.93, so it is accurate before you even touch calibration. Add Thunderbolt 4, 120Hz and a 5-star TUV EyeComfort rating and it is the 4K productivity monitor to beat. The ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays third as my pick for anyone who wants the best image quality on the list, with a stunning 4K OLED panel that is unmatched for contrast and motion. The Dell U3226Q remains the choice for those who want a larger 4K canvas with reference color, and the Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 keeps fifth for gamers who want OLED without the productivity focus. Further down, the ASUS ProArt PA279CRV and Gigabyte M28U continue to anchor the value end with strong color and price. Rankings hold steady because the field is mature and the new coverage reinforced existing positions rather than upending them.

highlights.body: 1440p IPS Black, a full Thunderbolt 4 hub, 120Hz and excellent ergonomics make it the most complete productivity display at its price. It is my first pick for most home offices.

highlights.title: Dell U2725QE confirmed as a contrast standout
highlights.body: RTINGS measured 2933:1 contrast at 58% brightness, exceptional for IPS. Combined with 0.93 dE out-of-box accuracy, it is the 4K productivity monitor to beat at number two.

highlights.title: U2725QE accuracy is calibration-free
highlights.body: 100% sRGB, 95% Adobe RGB and 92% DCI-P3 with a 0.93 dE average means it is accurate straight out of the box. Add a 5-star TUV EyeComfort rating for all-day comfort.

highlights.title: ASUS PG27UCDM owns image quality
highlights.body: Its 4K OLED panel is the best-looking display on this list for contrast and motion. If picture quality is your priority over a USB-C hub, this is the one to get.

highlights.body: The PA279CRV brings reference-grade color at a fair price, and the M28U remains a strong budget 4K pick. Both keep their spots as the smart-money options.

zh-tw.commentary: Dell U2724DE 還是穩坐我心中最適合多數人的全能螢幕第一名，這週也沒有任何事情改變這點。它的 1440p IPS Black 面板、完整的 Thunderbolt 4 集線功能、120Hz 更新率加上很棒的人體工學，讓它成為不用花大錢就能買到最完整的生產力螢幕。家裡的辦公空間或混合辦公的配置，我第一個還是推它。這週比較大的消息是 Dell U2725QE 的深度評測陸續出爐，這台 4K 兄弟機排在第二。RTINGS 跟其他媒體確認它是 Dell 第一款強化版 IPS Black 面板，在 58% 亮度時實測到 2933:1 的對比度，以 IPS 來說非常出色，黑色真的很深。它涵蓋 100% sRGB、95% Adobe RGB 跟 92% DCI-P3，出廠 dE 平均值 0.93，代表你還沒校色它就很準了。再加上 Thunderbolt 4、120Hz 跟 5 星 TUV 護眼認證，它就是 4K 生產力螢幕裡要被挑戰的對象。ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM 守第三，是我推薦給想要榜單裡最佳畫質的人，那塊 4K OLED 面板的對比跟動態表現無人能敵。Dell U3226Q 還是想要更大 4K 畫布又要參考級色彩的首選，Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 守第五，適合想要 OLED 又不需要生產力取向的玩家。再往下，ASUS ProArt PA279CRV 跟 Gigabyte M28U 繼續穩住高 CP 那端，色彩跟價格都很有競爭力。名次維持不動，因為這個市場很成熟，新出的評測是強化既有定位，而不是翻盤。

That is why I nudged its value score up. The bigger story is the OLED tier. Samsung's Odyssey OLED G8 dropped to around 657 dollars in this sale, a 47 percent cut, and that turns a premium splurge into a reasonable buy. I bumped its value and moved it level with the ASUS ProArt in my overall confidence, because a 4K QD-OLED at that price beats almost anything for mixed work and play. The Dell U3226Q remains the best image on this list, but it stays expensive even on sale, so its value still drags it to fourth. At the bottom, the Acer KB272 is the clearest Prime Day steal at 89 dollars, a 27 inch IPS panel that no one should overpay for, so its value score is now near maximum. My advice this week is simple. en.commentary: The Dell U2724DE stays my number one for the way most people actually work. A single USB-C cable powers the laptop, drives the display and runs the built-in KVM and Ethernet, so my desk stays clean and my meetings start on time. That practical productivity is worth more day to day than a spec sheet, which is why it beats flashier panels. Right behind it the U2725QE gives you the same docking smarts with a sharper 4K panel, and it is the one I recommend if text crispness matters to your eyes. Samsung's Odyssey OLED G8 remains my pick for gamers who want OLED contrast with high refresh, and the ASUS ProArt PA279CRV is still the value creator display I point people to when budget is tight but color work is not optional. New OLEDs keep landing this year, including Dell's July Alienware ultrawide, but nothing shipping today changes my order. Buy the U2724DE for a tidy productive desk. Step up to the U3226Q only if reference color is the job. The rest of the list is stable and I stand behind it.

en.commentary:
The Dell U2724DE keeps my top spot as the productivity monitor I recommend to the most people. The U2725QE holds second for anyone who wants that same hub experience at 4K sharpness.

The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays third on the strength of its 4K 240Hz QD-OLED panel, and the wider market backs that call: reviewers keep naming Asus QD-OLED as the top-tier gaming choice this year. What has changed around it is price. 4K OLED has gone mainstream, and you can now buy a stunning 32-inch 4K 240Hz panel for under $900, which reframes the whole value conversation even if it does not reorder my ranked set.

Dell's U3226Q remains the color-critical pick despite a steep price, and the Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 keeps its place for gamers who want that curved OLED punch. The ProArt PA279CRV stays my value creator pick with excellent color accuracy for the money. Down the list, the Gigabyte M28U and Acer KB272 still anchor the budget end. I held every rank steady this week. New arrivals like the 39-inch Alienware ultrawide are exciting, but they sit outside this set, so the order stands. If you want a productivity display today, the U2724DE is still the easy answer.

en.commentary:
The Dell U2724DE keeps the crown because it nails the thing most people actually buy a monitor for: a spacious, sharp, comfortable all-day workspace with a genuine hub built in. Its 90W-plus Thunderbolt connectivity means one cable powers a laptop and drives every peripheral, and that convenience earns it the top score even against flashier panels. For the office or a home desk, nothing else balances productivity and price this cleanly.

This week the story worth telling is at the high end. Fresh reviews of the Dell U3226Q QD-OLED landed, and Tom's Hardware called it pro-grade with reference-level color, praising its built-in automated colorimeter that calibrates itself while you step away. That is a real feature for anyone doing color-critical work, and it justifies the panel's place near the top of my color-accuracy column. If your income depends on repeatable color, this is the screen.

For gamers the ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM remains the image-quality standout, its 4K OLED delivering the kind of contrast and motion clarity that makes everything else look flat. The ProArt PA279CRV is still the value creator pick, and the Gigabyte M28U is the budget 4K workhorse I hand to anyone who wants sharp pixels without the OLED premium. Buy by use case and every one of these delivers.

en.commentary: My advice for productivity buyers has not moved this week: the Dell U2724DE remains the monitor I recommend first. The interesting news this month is at the high end, where Alienware confirmed the AW3926QW, a 39-inch 5K2K tandem WOLED landing in July around 1,100 dollars with peak brightness near 1,300 nits and a dual-mode 165Hz to 330Hz trick. That is aggressive pricing against the LG 39GX950B, and it signals that big 5K2K OLED is finally getting affordable enough to tempt creative and gaming crossover buyers. For color-critical work the Dell U3226Q and ASUS ProArt still anchor my picks, and the ASUS ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays the image-quality standout for gamers who want 4K OLED at speed. None of this reorders my current list, since those new ultrawides serve a different desk than the all-rounders up top. If you sit at a screen for eight hours, buy for the hub and the ergonomics. If you chase pixels, the new panels are worth watching closely.

en.commentary: The Dell UltraSharp U2724DE stays my number one for the same reason it has all year: it is the one monitor that does everything most people need without asking you to compromise. IPS Black contrast, a 120Hz panel that is smooth for everyday gaming, Thunderbolt 4 charging, and a built-in KVM at around $499 make it the display I recommend before any other. The bigger story this month is the OLED price collapse. Panels that sat above $1,500 a year ago are now landing under $900 for a 32-inch 4K 240Hz QD-OLED, and that shift is reshaping the whole middle of this list. The Dell U3226Q is the clearest beneficiary. Its 4K QD-OLED panel with a built-in colorimeter delivers reference-grade color, and as street prices ease I bumped its value score to 6.5. It is still a premium buy, but the gap to the mainstream is closing fast. The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM holds third as my pick for pure image quality, with the highest panel score in the group and the motion clarity competitive players want. The Samsung Odyssey OLED G8 stays a gorgeous gaming-first option at five. For anyone who values color work over gaming, the Asus ProArt PA279CRV at rank six remains the smartest spend, delivering factory-calibrated accuracy at a price that undercuts every other pro panel here. My rankings hold this week, but if you have been waiting to jump on 4K OLED, the pricing finally makes sense.

The Dell U2724DE stays number one because a built-in Thunderbolt hub, a 120Hz IPS panel and a superb stand solve the real daily friction of a work setup, one cable for power, video and data, and its connectivity score is the best here. The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM keeps third as the image-quality champion, its 4K 240Hz QD-OLED panel is stunning and it is the screen I recommend to gamers who also edit. Further down, the Gigabyte M28U and Acer KB272 keep the value lanes honest. I held the order steady this week because the buzzy new panels are ultrawide gaming displays that sit outside this productivity-first lineup. Decide whether you want a hub for work or a fast OLED for play, then everything else follows.

For a desk you use eight hours a day it is still the smart default. The ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays my gaming and mixed-use OLED pick at third, that panel is stunning and it doubles as a creator display. I left scores untouched this week because the lineup is stable and no new launch reshuffled the order. If you are color-critical and price is no object, the U3226Q is the aspirational buy, everyone else should still start with the U2724DE.

en.commentary: The Dell UltraSharp U2724DE stays my overall recommendation because it is the display that does the most for the most people. IPS Black gives it deep, even contrast for daily work, the 120 hertz panel is smooth enough for after-hours gaming, and the Thunderbolt 4 charging with a built-in KVM turns it into a genuine one-cable desk hub at around five hundred dollars. That combination is why reviewers keep naming it the best all-rounder, and I agree. The U2725QE holds second as the 4K step up for anyone who wants the extra sharpness on a 27 inch panel with the same excellent connectivity. The Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM keeps third on pure image quality, its QD-OLED panel is the one I reach for when motion clarity and contrast matter more than desktop real estate. This month the buzz was all about new hardware on the horizon. Dell announced the Alienware AW3926QW, a 39 inch 5K2K tandem WOLED aimed at July, and Asus continued rolling out fresh QD-OLED panels, so the high end is about to get more crowded. None of that is shipping in volume yet, so I am holding my ranking steady, but it is a good reminder to buy for the panel in front of you rather than the one in a press release. For most desks the value picks still win. The Asus ProArt PA279CRV and the true 5K ProArt line remain the smart choice for color work without the flagship premium.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週螢幕新聞熱鬧，但動作都在我榜單旁邊。Dell 在 7 月 14 日推出 Alienware AW3426DW，34 吋 280Hz QD-OLED，擺明衝玩家來，那是好面板，只是落在這份生產力為主的排名之外，我追蹤的機種名次都守住。Dell U2724DE 維持第一，生產力該有的都到位，銳利面板、90 瓦以上 USB-C 完整擴充座、走到哪都能喬的腳架，價格又讓它成為混合辦公最安全的預設值。U2725QE 守第二，是同家族的 4K 進階版，看重文字銳利度這錢花得值。Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM 靠純畫質排第三，那塊 4K QD-OLED 仍是玩家和影音迷心心念念的面板。U3226Q 排第四，出廠校準 4K QD-OLED 加抗眩光，創作專業戶該列名單。再往下 ProArt PA279CRV 是性價比創作螢幕，Gigabyte M28U 是預算 4K 遊戲首選，Acer KB272 是台幣三千內辦公主力。最新 Dell OLED 落在遊戲線，這週名次全留。

highlights.body: 4K QD-OLED 仍是玩家和影音迷最想要的面板，即使在生產力榜也靠純畫質排第三。

references.title: Dell's 2026 monitors: 4K QD-OLED for professionals and first 52-inch 6K LCD
Dell UltraSharp 32 4K QD-OLED Monitor (U3226Q)
OLED Monitors In 2026: Current Market Status

en.commentary: The Dell UltraSharp U2724DE stays my top all-rounder this July. For a productivity desk it does everything right: a 120Hz IPS Black panel with deep contrast, a full Thunderbolt hub with 90W laptop charging, and an ergonomic stand that actually adjusts the way you want. At its price it remains the monitor I recommend to the most people. The U2725QE holds second as the 4K version of that same recipe for anyone who wants sharper text for code and photo work. Third goes to the Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM, still the gaming showpiece with its 4K QD-OLED panel that looks stunning in a dark room. The story worth flagging this month is Dell's own U3226Q sitting at number four. Dell confirmed it as the world's first QD-OLED monitor with an anti-glare low-reflectance coating, a 32-inch 4K panel factory calibrated below 1 DeltaE with a built-in recalibration sensor. That anti-glare treatment solves the one thing that has kept glossy OLEDs off bright creative desks, so it is the panel I am watching most closely as it reaches buyers. I held its rank and score steady this week because availability is still ramping, but it earns its 9.8 color accuracy. Further down, the Asus ProArt PA279CRV is my value creator pick and the Gigabyte M28U is the budget 4K gaming choice. Buy the U2724DE for work, the PG27UCDM for play.

highlights.title: U2724DE is the all-rounder
highlights.body: A 120Hz IPS Black panel, a full Thunderbolt hub with 90W charging, and a genuinely adjustable stand keep this my pick for the most desks.

highlights.title: Dell's anti-glare OLED arrives
highlights.body: The U3226Q is confirmed as the world's first QD-OLED with anti-glare low-reflectance coating, a 32-inch 4K panel calibrated below 1 DeltaE. It earns a 9.8 for color accuracy.

highlights.title: The gaming showpiece
highlights.body: Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM stays third on its 4K QD-OLED panel, which looks stunning in a dark room and posts a 9.9 for image quality.

highlights.title: Value picks
highlights.body: Asus ProArt PA279CRV is my value choice for creators at a 9.0 value score, and the Gigabyte M28U is the budget 4K gaming monitor.

zh-tw.commentary: Dell UltraSharp U2724DE 這個七月還是我心中最強的全能款。當生產力桌面來用，它每件事都做對了，120Hz 的 IPS Black 面板對比夠深，完整的 Thunderbolt 集線器還能給筆電 90W 充電，腳架的升降旋轉也真的調得到你想要的角度。以它這個價位，還是我推薦給最多人的螢幕。U2725QE 穩坐第二，等於是同一套配方的 4K 版本，寫程式或修圖想要更銳利的字就選它。第三名是 Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM，依舊是電競門面，4K QD-OLED 面板在暗房裡看真的很驚艷。這個月值得特別點名的，是 Dell 自家排第四的 U3226Q。Dell 確認它是全球第一台採用抗眩光低反射鍍膜的 QD-OLED，32 吋 4K 面板出廠校色壓在 1 DeltaE 以內，還內建重新校色的感測器。這個抗眩光處理解決了亮面 OLED 一直上不了明亮創作桌的那個痛點，所以它正式鋪貨後我會最緊盯著看。這週我先把它的名次跟分數維持原樣，因為供貨還在爬坡，但它色準拿到 9.8 是實至名歸。再往下，Asus ProArt PA279CRV 是我的創作者性價比之選，Gigabyte M28U 則是預算型 4K 電競的選擇。工作買 U2724DE，玩遊戲買 PG27UCDM。

highlights.title: U2724DE 是全能王
highlights.body: 120Hz IPS Black 面板、完整 Thunderbolt 集線器加 90W 充電、真的調得到位的腳架，讓它一直是我推薦給最多桌面的螢幕。

highlights.title: Dell 抗眩光 OLED 登場
highlights.body: U3226Q 確認是全球第一台抗眩光低反射鍍膜的 QD-OLED，32 吋 4K 面板校色壓在 1 DeltaE 以內，色準拿到 9.8。

highlights.title: 電競門面
highlights.body: Asus ROG Swift PG27UCDM 靠 4K QD-OLED 面板穩坐第三，暗房裡看超驚艷，畫質分數拿到 9.9。

highlights.title: 性價比之選
highlights.body: Asus ProArt PA279CRV 是我給創作者的性價比選擇，價值分數 9.0；Gigabyte M28U 則是預算型 4K 電競螢幕。
